article.description: — LeBron James made two free throws with 3.9 seconds left, Luka Doncic scored 29 points and the Los Angeles Lakers recovered from blowing a 20-point lead in the fourth quarter to top the Phoenix Suns 116-114 on Sunday night.
article.headline: LeBron James hits 2 late free throws, Lakers beat Suns 116-114 after blowing 20-point lead

images.caption: LeBron James is assessed an unsportsmanlike technical foul for his dustup with Dillon Brooks.
images.name: LeBron gets T'd up after jawing with Dillon Brooks

article.linkText: LeBron James hits 2 late free throws, Lakers beat Suns 116-114 after blowing 20-point lead
article.story: PHOENIX -- — LeBron James made two free throws with 3.9 seconds left, Luka Doncic scored 29 points and the Los Angeles Lakers recovered from blowing a 20-point lead in the fourth quarter to top the Phoenix Suns 116-114 on Sunday night. Phoenix trailed 99-79 with 7:48 left, but took a 114-113 lead with 12.2 seconds left on Dillon Brooks' 3-pointer over James, who made contact with Brooks after the shot. Brooks bumped James on the way back down the court, earning his second technical foul and an ejection. More importantly, it gave the Lakers a free throw, but James missed it. On the ensuing possession, James was fouled on a 3-point attempt by Devin Booker with 3.9 seconds left. The 40-year-old James — who finished with 26 points — missed the first free throw but made the final two to give the Lakers a 115-114 lead. Phoenix's Grayson Allen got up an awkward shot at the buzzer, but it was off the mark. Jaxson Hayes threw down a powerful dunk over Phoenix's Oso Ighodaro to give the Lakers a 79-77 lead with 2:24 left in the third quarter. It was part of a 24-0 run over an eight-minute span in the third and fourth quarters that ended with a 95-77 advantage. Lakers center Deandre Ayton had 20 points and 13 rebounds against his former team. Ayton played his first five NBA seasons in Phoenix after being selected with the No. 1 overall pick in 2018. Los Angeles had a season-high 24 offensive rebounds, which helped offset a tough shooting night. Doncic was 7 of 25 from the field, including 2 of 14 from 3-point range. Booker led the Suns with 27 points, returning to the lineup after missing three games with a groin injury. The four-time All-Star made 13 of 16 free throws. The Suns have lost six of their last nine. Up next Lakers: At Utah on Thursday night. Suns: Host Golden State on Thursday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

videos.description: Dillon Brooks gives the Suns the late lead with a 3-pointer after rejecting Marcus Smart to start a fast break for Phoenix.

videos.description: Dillon Brooks receives his second technical foul for making contact with LeBron James after hitting a go-ahead 3-pointer for the Suns.

videos.description: Devin Booker sends LeBron James to the free-throw line with less than a minute left allowing the Lakers to take the lead for good.
videos.headline: Booker commits costly foul on LeBron's 3-point attempt
